actor: OCC supervisory-policy staff drafting the agentic-AI Model Risk Management addendum (Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, Risk Analytics Division)
job: draft an addendum to OCC 2011-12 / SR 11-7 that explicitly names agent-based decisioning, MCP-mediated tool composition, and chained core-system actions as in-scope model-risk activities, with audit-trail, human-in-the-loop, and SAR-narrative-attribution requirements aligned to the May 4 FIS + Anthropic AML Financial Crimes Agent shape and the May 5 Anthropic 10-agent finance launch.
outcome: Published OCC bulletin (addendum or companion guidance to OCC 2011-12) naming agentic AI as a distinct model-risk category with explicit examiner expectations on audit trail, model card, and SAR-narrative attribution.
motivation: BMO is OCC-supervised at the US holdco level and is named as a first-deployment customer for the FIS-Anthropic AML agent; examiners need a published framework before the H2 2026 broader-availability rollout lands inside more OCC-supervised banks.

task.who: OCC Risk Analytics Division + Office of the Chief National Bank Examiner + Bank Supervision Policy + interagency liaisons to FRB and FDIC
task.what: draft the agentic-AI MRM addendum, run interagency comment, and publish as an OCC bulletin with examiner handbook updates
task.where: OCC headquarters (Washington DC) + interagency drafting sessions with FRB Division of Supervision and Regulation
task.why: SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 was written for static models; agentic systems chain tool calls and external MCP data brokers in minutes, leaving examiners without a clean framework for Claude-driven AML pipelines
task.how: publish as a supervisory bulletin (companion to OCC 2011-12) with examiner handbook annexes naming audit-trail, human-in-the-loop, and SAR-narrative-attribution control families

actor: large-bank Chief Risk Officer + Model Risk Management lead at an OCC-supervised institution (BMO US, Citizens, KeyBank, Truist, US Bank, Wells Fargo)
job: stand up an examiner-ready agentic-AI control inventory covering the FIS-Anthropic AML agent, the Anthropic 10-agent finance suite (pitch builder, earnings reviewer, KYC screener, statement auditor, month-end closer, general ledger reconciler), and the MCP connectors (Moody's, Dun & Bradstreet, Verisk, IBISWorld) so the bank can demonstrate compliance the day the OCC addendum publishes.
outcome: Internal MRM register that maps every agentic-AI workflow to a model card, audit-trail logging endpoint, human-in-the-loop checkpoint, and SAR-narrative-attribution log, ready for on-cycle examiner review.
motivation: First-mover examiner readiness avoids a Matter Requiring Attention finding and protects the bank's ability to keep using the FIS-Anthropic stack through the H2 2026 broader-availability rollout.

task.who: CRO + Head of Model Risk Management + Chief Compliance Officer + BSA/AML officer + Chief Data Officer
task.what: build an agentic-AI control inventory, draft model cards for each agent workflow, and run a pre-examination tabletop with internal audit
task.where: bank's model risk committee + AML governance committee + technology risk forum + OCC examiner liaison meetings
task.why: BMO and Amalgamated Bank are first deployment customers for the FIS-Anthropic AML agent and broader availability targets H2 2026, so OCC examiner cycles will land on agentic-AI workloads before year end
task.how: extend existing SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 model risk registers with agent-workflow rows, link to audit-trail logging endpoints, and require SAR-narrative-attribution logs for every Claude-drafted SAR

actor: Anthropic + FIS regulatory affairs + product team building examiner-grade audit-trail and model-card features for the AML Financial Crimes Agent
job: ship a regulator-readable evidence pack — audit trail of every MCP tool call, model card per agent, SAR-narrative-attribution log, and human-in-the-loop checkpoint catalogue — that aligns with the OCC addendum's expected control families before H2 2026 broader availability.
outcome: Publicly documented regulator-grade audit-trail + model-card feature set on the FIS-Anthropic AML agent, referenced in customer examiner-readiness packets at BMO, Amalgamated, and the H2 2026 onboarding cohort.
motivation: The May 4 BusinessWire announcement already named regulator-grade audit trail as a feature; the OCC addendum makes that promise testable, and a vendor that fails the first examiner cycle loses the H2 2026 expansion pipeline.

task.who: Anthropic financial-services product manager + FIS compliance product team + outside counsel (Davis Polk / Sullivan & Cromwell) + customer success engineers at BMO and Amalgamated
task.what: harden audit-trail logging across MCP connectors, publish per-agent model cards, ship a SAR-narrative-attribution log, and document human-in-the-loop checkpoints
task.where: joint Anthropic + FIS engineering channels + customer-deployment teams at BMO and Amalgamated + regulatory-affairs working sessions
task.why: FIS powers ~12% of the global economy and the AML agent auto-assembles SAR evidence + drafts SAR narratives across a bank's core systems, so the audit-trail must be examiner-readable end-to-end
task.how: instrument every MCP tool call (Dun & Bradstreet, Moody's, Verisk, IBISWorld, Fiscal AI, SS&C IntraLinks, Third Bridge) with structured audit logs; publish model cards per agent role; ship a SAR-narrative-attribution log feature
